title: The Space Program In The Eva Verse
display_title: The Space Program In The Eva Verse
wikitext:
[]: The Space Program In The Eva-Verse             Quote Post by Ray » Thu Feb 20, 2014 4:47 pm Hello, my name is Ray. Been an Eva fan for a long time but only joined the site recently. Sup? Anyway, there is one flaw in the entire plot of Evangelion that I have a hard time thinking up a solution to. The issue is, if Aliens really were invading, like the Angels in the series were with intent on merging all souls on planet earth into one. Why was no effort put into creating a means to ensure mankind's survival in the event the Angels won? Why was no effort put into establishing a viable space program? True there were armed satellites, and even a fully functioning moonbase in the Rebuilds. But never anything beyond that. Why? Was post 2nd Impact Earth that tapped for resources? If the Angels were only attacking earth and only wanting to destroy earth, then why was no effort put into making sure not every human was on earth? A colony on Mars, or a satellite orbiting beyond the angels reach should have been sufficient to ensure mankind's survival in the event instrumentality happened. You'd think ensuring the survival of the species would have been a priority! Give your reasons for this conundrum below.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Feb 20, 2014 4:58 pm Because it's a story about characters dealing with world-ending/world-saving shenanigans. It's not actually about saving the world. I've long wanted Eva to progress into a full-blown space opera (and Q adds delicious potential) but that'll probably never be the focus of the main work.

[]: Re: The Space Program In The Eva-Verse             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Feb 20, 2014 5:07 pm Ray wrote: True there were armed satellites, and even a fully functioning moonbase in the Rebuilds. But never anything beyond that. Why? Was post 2nd Impact Earth that tapped for resources?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Feb 20, 2014 6:04 pm The only bit of interesting new aerospace kit we see is the spaceplane Gendo uses in one of his bits of off-site conspiracy. There is that convenient single unit shuttle belly that gets pressed into ad hoc service in Operation Yashima, but no sign of the rest of it. So it's probably safe to assume an optimistic twenty year projection for aerospace, but the new Manhattan Project is Project E. The Space 1999 bits of Ha were one of the WTFs for me when the trailers came out -- a miracle too far.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Feb 20, 2014 6:28 pm Mr. Tines wrote: The Space 1999 bits of Ha were one of the WTFs for me when the trailers came out -- a miracle too far.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Feb 21, 2014 4:02 pm On top of what everyone else said, the people who were basically in control of mankind's future had no interest in preserving the human species. It was SEELE that wanted to fuse all human souls into one, not the Angels. The Angels just wanted their mom back, and were more-or-less slaughtered innocents for SEELE's religious symbolism high.

Article: The Space Program In The Eva Verse
 only bit of interesting new aerospace kit we see is the spaceplane Gendo uses in one of his bits of off-site conspiracy. There is that convenient single unit shuttle belly that gets pressed into ad hoc service in Operation Yashima, but no sign of the rest of it. So it's probably safe to assume an optimistic twenty year projection for aerospace, but the new Manhattan Project is Project E. The Space 1999 bits of Ha were one of the WTFs for me when the trailers came out -- a miracle too far.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Feb 20, 2014 6:28 pm Mr. Tines wrote: The Space 1999 bits of Ha were one of the WTFs for me when the trailers came out -- a miracle too far.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Feb 21, 2014 4:02 pm On top of what everyone else said, the people who were basically in control of mankind's future had no interest in preserving the human species. It was SEELE that wanted to fuse all human souls into one, not the Angels. The Angels just wanted their mom back, and were more-or-less slaughtered innocents for SEELE's religious symbolism high.
